timber.definition: Trees in a forest regarded as a source of wood.

timely.definition: Done at the proper time or within the proper time limits; prompt.

tobacco.definition: Any plant of the genus Nicotiana.

tolerance.definition: The ability to endure pain or hardship; endurance.

toll.definition: Loss or damage incurred through a disaster.

top.definition: The highest or uppermost part of something.

torture.definition: Intentional causing of somebody's experiencing agony

toss.definition: A throw, a lob, of a ball etc., with an initial upward direction, particularly with a lack of care.

total.definition: An amount obtained by the addition of smaller amounts.

toxic.definition: Having a chemical nature that is harmful to health or lethal if consumed or otherwise entering into the body in sufficient quantities.

trademark.definition: A word, symbol, or phrase used to identify a particular company's product and differentiate it from other companies' products.

trail.definition: The track or indication marking the route followed by something that has passed, such as the footprints of animal on land or the contrail of an airplane in the sky.

trailer.definition: Someone who or something that trails.

transaction.definition: The act of conducting or carrying out (business, negotiations, plans).

transcript.definition: Something which has been transcribed; a writing or composition consisting of the same words as the original; a written copy.

transformation.definition: The act of transforming or the state of being transformed.

transit.definition: The act of passing over, across, or through something.

transmission.definition: The act of transmitting, e.g. data or electric power.

transparency.definition: The quality of being transparent; transparence.

transparent.definition: (of a material or object) See-through, clear; having the property that light passes through it almost undisturbed, such that one can see through it clearly.

trauma.definition: Any serious injury to the body, often resulting from violence or an accident.

treaty.definition: A binding agreement concluded by subjects of international law, namely states and international organizations.

tribal.definition: A design or image that has been influenced by indigenous peoples; especially such a tattoo.

tribute.definition: An acknowledgment of gratitude, respect or admiration; an accompanying gift.

trio.definition: A group of three people or things.

triumph.definition: A conclusive success following an effort, conflict, or confrontation of obstacles; victory; conquest.

troubled.definition: To disturb, stir up, agitate (a medium, especially water).

trustee.definition: A person to whom property is legally committed in trust, to be applied either for the benefit of specified individuals, or for public uses; one who is intrusted with property for the benefit of another.

tuition.definition: A sum of money paid for instruction (such as in a high school, boarding school, university, or college).

tumour.definition: An abnormal growth; differential diagnosis includes abscess, metaplasia, and neoplasia.

turnover.definition: The amount of money taken as sales transacted in a given period.

U.definition: The name of the Latin-script letter U.

unconstitutional.definition: Contrary to or violative of the constitution of a state or other sovereign institution

undergraduate.definition: A student at a university who has not yet received a degree.

underlying.definition: To lie in a position directly beneath.

undermine.definition: To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.

unify.definition: Cause to become one; make into a unit; consolidate; merge; combine.

unprecedented.definition: Never before seen, done, or experienced; without precedent.

unveil.definition: To remove a veil from; to uncover; to reveal something hidden.

uphold.definition: To hold up; to lift on high; to elevate.

utility.definition: The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.

utilise.definition: To make use of; to use.

utterly.definition: Completely, entirely, to the fullest extent

V.definition: A shape resembling the letter v

vacuum.definition: A region of space that contains no matter.

validity.definition: The state of being valid, authentic or genuine.

vanish.definition: The brief terminal part of a vowel or vocal element, differing more or less in quality from the main part.

vein.definition: A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.

venture.definition: A risky or daring undertaking or journey.

verbal.definition: (grammar) A verb form which does not function as a predicate, or a word derived from a verb. In English, infinitives, participles and gerunds are verbals.

verdict.definition: A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.

verify.definition: To substantiate or prove the truth of something

verse.definition: A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.

vessel.definition: Any craft designed for transportation on water, such as a ship or boat.

veteran.definition: A person with long experience of a particular activity.

viable.definition: An organism that is able to live and develop.

violate.definition: To break or disregard (a rule or convention).

violation.definition: The act or an instance of violating or the condition of being violated.

virtue.definition: Accordance with moral principles; conformity of behaviour or thought with the strictures of morality; good moral conduct.

vocal.definition: A vocal sound; specifically, a purely vocal element of speech, unmodified except by resonance; a vowel or a diphthong; a tonic element; a tonic; distinguished from a subvocal, and a nonvocal

vow.definition: A solemn promise to perform some act, or behave in a specified manner, especially a promise to live and act in accordance with the rules of a religious order.

vulnerability.definition: Susceptibility to attack or injury; the state or condition of being weak or poorly defended.

vulnerable.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.

ward.definition: A warden; a guard; a guardian or watchman.

warehouse.definition: A place for storing large amounts of products. In logistics, a place where products go to from the manufacturer before going to the retailer.

warfare.definition: The waging of war or armed conflict against an enemy.

warrant.definition: Authorization or certification; a sanction, as given by a superior.

warrior.definition: A person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier or combatant.

weaken.definition: To make weaker or less strong.

weave.definition: A type or way of weaving.

weed.definition: Any plant regarded as unwanted at the place where, and at the time when it is growing.

whereby.definition: (interrogative) By what, in which direction; how.

whip.definition: A lash; a pliant, flexible instrument, such as a rod (commonly of cane or rattan) or a plaited or braided rope or thong (commonly of leather) used to create a sharp "crack" sound for directing or herding animals.

wholly.definition: Completely and entirely; to the fullest extent.

widow.definition: A woman whose spouse has died (and who has not remarried); feminine of widower.

withdrawal.definition: Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.
